articles.title: Nearby hospitals, emergency contacts displayed at Delhi shelter homes to aid homeless during heatwave
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Nearby hospitals, emergency contacts displayed at Delhi shelter homes to aid homeless during heatwave
Nearby hospitals, emergency contacts displayed at Delhi shelter homes to aid homeless during heatwave
New Delhi, The Delhi Urban Shelter Improvement Board has pinpointed the location of government hospitals situated near shelter homes and installed QR codes for the Rain Basera app at shelters across the capital, to help inhabitants access emergency assistance during extreme weather conditions, officials said on Saturday.
Banners displaying details of nearby hospitals, emergency response numbers, and the contact for DUSIB's summer control room has been put up at shelters to ensure timely medical help in case of emergencies, they said.
In view of imminent heatwave conditions across the capital, the DUSIB has intensified measures to protect the homeless population residing in shelter homes across Delhi.
According to officials, all shelter homes are providing basic facilities, including three meals a day, sanitation, drinking water, and bedding to inhabitants.
Shelters have been equipped with water dispensers or coolers, ceiling fans, desert coolers, exhaust fans, and mosquito repellents, they said.
As part of its summer action plan, the DUSIB is distributing ORS packets in shelter homes, while posters regarding availability of free ORS have been put up to create awareness among residents.
"Proper hydration for the residents is being ensured. Minor repair work of electrical equipments installed in these facilities is also being carried out and will be completed next week," a senior DUSIB official said.
Officials said awareness and sensitisation activities are being conducted to educate residents about heatwave conditions, hydration, and precautions against heat-related illnesses.
The "Do's and Don'ts" related to heatwaves have also been displayed at shelter homes for wider awareness and preparedness, they added.
The DUSIB operates 197 shelters across the capital with a combined capacity of nearly 17,286 people.
According to official data, 82 shelters function from permanent reinforced concrete structures while 115 operate out of portable cabins.
The shelters have an average annual occupancy of around 5,500 people, as most of those availing night shelters are migrants and homeless persons.
This article was generated from an automated news agency feed without modifications to text.

articles.title: Delhi government sets up cooling zones to ease heatwave impact - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Amid a severe heatwave affecting New Delhi, the Delhi Government has established ‘Cooling Zones’ in crowded locations such as bus stands and metro stations to offer relief to the public. These camps are managed with assistance from the Delhi Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) and Civil Defence volunteers to help people endure the intense summer heat.
“At these centres, ORS water, normal water and cold drinking water are available. We also have coolers and fans. Due to the extreme heat, the Delhi Government has set up these Cooling Zones, and we are assisting in their operation. People can sit here and rest for 10 to 15 minutes,” said Jeet Pal, a member of DDMA.
Civil Defence and Delhi Police personnel are overseeing the smooth operation of the sites. “This facility has been provided by the government for the public. We have caps, ORS and cold water available for people. All basic facilities are here so that anyone can sit and take rest,” stated Uzma, a member of Civil Defence.
Residents have welcomed the initiative as a timely measure during peak summer conditions. “This is a very good initiative. Delhi faces extreme heat and frequent heat waves. People are getting relief here with ORS, cold water and a place to sit for some time,” said local resident Rehan Ali.
Another commuter, Uttam Singh, added, “We were passing by and saw this Cooling Zone. It is very useful for the public. People travelling on the road can rest here comfortably in the heat.”
The initiative is becoming a public service example, offering immediate relief and essential first-aid support to those facing severe summer conditions in the city.
